basics.name: Aden Guo
basics.label: Geomatics Engineering Graduate
basics.summary: Geomatics engineering graduate with hands-on experience in photogrammetry, geospatial data analysis, and field survey workflows. Builds practical data and simulation pipelines using Python/C++ and communicates results through technical reporting and presentations.

work.name: University of Calgary
work.summary: Led a team project evaluating surveying methods for disc golf course mapping and delivered final technical findings.
highlights: Compared RTK GNSS, range finder, and odometer techniques to assess mapping accuracy and efficiency.
Operated drones for photogrammetric capture and executed spatial intersection workflows for 3D modeling.
Processed and validated geospatial datasets with photogrammetry tools and Python-based analysis pipelines.
Authored and presented the final report at the SaGES Project Presentation.

work.name: Gongcha
work.summary: Managed high-volume shift operations while training team members on standardized service workflows.
highlights: Trained and mentored staff to improve consistency and teamwork.
Balanced service demands and operational tasks under time constraints.
Strengthened team coordination, problem-solving, and day-to-day execution.

courses: Photogrammetry, 3D modeling, and digital imaging workflows
Geospatial data analysis, error modeling, and quality control
Spatial database design, SQL, and geospatial data mining
Project management, technical reporting, and collaborative problem-solving
Applied geomatics workflow through capstone and ENGO 500 field survey camp

projects.name: Photogrammetry Pipeline - Bundle Adjustment & Spatial Intersection
projects.description: Modular photogrammetry workflow using synthetic and real-world datasets for spatial intersection and bundle adjustment.
highlights: Implemented camera models, pose estimation, and 3D reconstruction with C++ and Python.
Built residual, optimization, and quality-assessment components to improve reconstruction accuracy.

projects.name: ML-Driven Sensor Simulation + EKF Sensor Fusion
projects.description: Simulation framework for IMU/GNSS trajectory generation with machine learning and EKF fusion.
highlights: Developing RNN/Transformer-based models for trajectory simulation.
Integrating EKF for real-time estimation of pose, velocity, and orientation.
Designing modular training, visualization, and evaluation pipelines.
